name: UCLA Consortium for Neuropsychiatric Phenomics (CNP)
description: Multimodal MRI from 272 participants — healthy adults and people with schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, or ADHD — performing tasks including emotional face matching, social and affective processing, working memory, and response inhibition. A widely used open dataset for linking dimensional psychiatric phenotypes to brain function, including social and emotional processing.
citation: Poldrack RA, Congdon E, Triplett W, et al. (2016). A phenome-wide examination of neural and cognitive function (UCLA Consortium for Neuropsychiatric Phenomics LA5c Study). Scientific Data, 3, 160110.
